title.zh: 悬圃的司神，四海的巡使
title.en: Keeper of the Hanging Garden, Herald of the Four Seas

summary.zh: 槐江之山天帝平圃的司神，马身人面、虎纹鸟翼，巡行四海传布天帝之命。
summary.en: Tutelary god of the High God's garden on Mount Huaijiang: horse-bodied and human-faced, tiger-striped and bird-winged, patrolling the four seas with heaven's commands.

description.zh:
槐江之山“实惟帝之平圃”——天帝设在人间的园圃，即后世所称的悬圃、玄圃，由神英招掌管。《西次三经》写他“其状马身而人面，虎文而鸟翼，徇于四海，其音如榴”：马的身体，人的面孔，皮毛带虎纹，背生鸟翼，巡行四海传布天帝的命令，声音如同辘轳抽水。

从槐江之山南望便是昆仑，“其光熊熊，其气魂魂”；西望大泽，是后稷神灵所潜之处；山上蕴藏黄金、雄黄与琅玕。平圃正处在俯瞰昆仑的高台上。《淮南子·墬形训》把悬圃列为昆仑通天阶梯的一级：登上它便“登之乃灵，能使风雨”。

英招与守昆仑的陆吾同为西部群山中人面兽身的司神：陆吾坐镇天帝的下都，英招看守天帝的园圃并巡行四海，一静一动，分掌天帝在下界的两处禁地。
description.en:
Mount Huaijiang 'is in truth the level garden of the High God' — the paradise later ages called the Hanging Garden — and the god Yingzhao keeps it. The 'Classic of the Western Mountains' gives him a horse's body and a human face, tiger stripes and bird's wings; he 'makes his circuit of the four seas', proclaiming the God's commands, with a voice like water drawn through a pipe.

From Huaijiang one looks south to Kunlun, 'its light blazing, its vapors billowing'; westward lies the great marsh where the spirit of Houji, lord of millet, lies hidden; the mountain itself yields gold, realgar, and langgan gems. The garden thus sits on a terrace overlooking Kunlun, and the Huainanzi counts the Hanging Garden as one rung of the ladder that leads up the mountain to heaven — whoever climbs it 'becomes numinous, able to command wind and rain'.

Yingzhao pairs naturally with Luwu, warden of Kunlun: both are human-faced, beast-bodied tutelary gods of the far west, one seated in the God's capital, the other ranging out from the God's garden across the four seas.

value.en: Horse's body, human face, tiger stripes, bird's wings

value.zh: 掌管帝之平圃，徇于四海
value.en: Keeper of the God's garden, herald to the four seas

image.sourceTitle: Imperial Encyclopaedia - Spirits and the Supernatural - pic14 - 英招神圖
